rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid '<error>' type issues.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all type references match AADL data types (int, real, bool) and are properly declared.
Validate that AGREE expressions resolve to valid types; replace invalid clauses with correct ones or remove them.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation contracts in implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for helper declarations and `gua` for guarantees; avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level scopes; use assign for output bindings.
Do not reference undefined identifiers or invalid types in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
